entries.name: Go — Effective Go
entries.description: Guía oficial de estilo y convenciones para código Go idiomático: nombrado, errores, goroutines y paquetes.
entries.excerpt: Nombrá con MixedCaps, no snake_case. Manejá errores explícitamente — nunca ignorés un error con _. Usá goroutines con defer para garantizar cleanup. Escribí comentarios godoc en cada símbolo exportado comenzando con su nombre.

entries.name: Go — Gin HTTP Framework
entries.description: Convenciones de routing, middleware y manejo de errores para aplicaciones HTTP con Gin.
entries.excerpt: Usá gin.Context.ShouldBind para validar request bodies. Centralizá el manejo de errores con un middleware ErrorHandler. Agrupá rutas con router.Group para prefijos y middlewares compartidos. Preferí c.JSON sobre c.String para APIs.

entries.name: React — Rules of React
entries.description: Reglas oficiales de React sobre componentes puros, Hooks y efectos secundarios.
entries.excerpt: Los componentes y Hooks deben ser funciones puras: mismas entradas → misma salida. Nunca mutés props ni state directamente. Llamá Hooks solo en el top-level — nunca dentro de condicionales o loops. Los efectos son para sincronización con sistemas externos, no para cómputo derivado.

entries.name: Next.js — App Router Conventions
entries.description: Convenciones del App Router de Next.js: layouts, Server Components, streaming y data fetching.
entries.excerpt: Por defecto todos los componentes en app/ son Server Components — solo agregá 'use client' cuando necesites interactividad o APIs del browser. Usá fetch() en Server Components para data fetching: Next lo cachea y deduplica automáticamente. Layouts anidan automáticamente sin re-render.

entries.name: Vue.js — Style Guide
entries.description: Guía oficial de estilo para componentes Vue 3: naming, Composition API y estructura de archivos SFC.
entries.excerpt: Usá Composition API con <script setup> para componentes nuevos. Los nombres de componentes deben ser siempre multi-palabra para evitar conflictos con HTML. Definí props con defineProps y tipos explícitos. Evitá acceder a this en setup().

entries.name: Node.js — Express Best Practices
entries.description: Mejores prácticas de Express.js: manejo de errores asíncrono, middleware y seguridad.
entries.excerpt: Usá async/await con un wrapper para capturar errores y pasarlos a next(err). Definí un middleware de error con 4 parámetros (err, req, res, next) al final. Nunca confiés en el body sin validarlo. Activá compression y helmet en producción.

entries.name: Python — PEP 8 Style Guide
entries.description: Convenciones de estilo oficial de Python: indentación, imports, nomenclatura y longitud de línea.
entries.excerpt: Usá 4 espacios para indentación, nunca tabs. Máximo 79 caracteres por línea. snake_case para funciones y variables, PascalCase para clases, UPPER_CASE para constantes. Imports en orden: stdlib → third-party → local, separados por línea en blanco.

entries.name: Django — Best Practices
entries.description: Convenciones oficiales de Django: ORM, views basadas en clase, señales y seguridad.
entries.excerpt: Usá select_related y prefetch_related para evitar N+1 queries. Preferí Class-Based Views para operaciones CRUD estándar. Nunca confíes en datos del usuario: usá always_escape y Form/Serializer para validación. Activá CSRF en todos los formularios POST.

entries.name: FastAPI — Patterns & Best Practices
entries.description: Patrones de FastAPI: dependency injection, modelos Pydantic, async handlers y documentación automática.
entries.excerpt: Usá Pydantic models para request/response: validación automática y documentación OpenAPI incluida. Inyectá dependencias con Depends() — DB sessions, auth, settings. Decorá con async def cuando usés operaciones I/O asíncronas. Response models ocultan campos internos del schema público.

entries.name: Rust — The Book: Ownership & Safety
entries.description: Reglas de ownership, borrowing y lifetimes del lenguaje Rust para código seguro sin GC.
entries.excerpt: Cada valor tiene un único owner; cuando sale de scope se libera. Podés tener muchas referencias inmutables (&T) o una sola mutable (&mut T) — nunca ambas simultáneamente. Usá Result<T,E> para errores recuperables y panic! solo para bugs irrecuperables. Preferí iteradores sobre loops manuales.

entries.name: Docker — Dockerfile Best Practices
entries.description: Mejores prácticas oficiales para Dockerfiles: capas, multi-stage builds, usuarios no-root y tamaño de imagen.
entries.excerpt: Usá multi-stage builds para separar build de runtime y reducir el tamaño final. Agrupá RUN commands con && para minimizar capas. Copiá primero solo los archivos de dependencias (package.json, go.mod) para aprovechar el cache. Nunca corras el contenedor como root — usá USER.

entries.name: TypeScript — Handbook: Type System
entries.description: Reglas del sistema de tipos de TypeScript: strict mode, utility types, type narrowing y generics.
entries.excerpt: Activá strict: true en tsconfig para habilitar strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny y strictFunctionTypes. Usá unknown en lugar de any para inputs no confiables — forzá type narrowing explícito. Preferí interfaces para formas de objetos y type aliases para uniones/intersecciones. Evitá type assertions (as X) — usá type guards.

entries.name: Rust — Clippy Lints
entries.description: Lints y correcciones idiomáticas de Clippy: código más claro, eficiente y seguro en Rust.
entries.excerpt: Usá cargo clippy -- -D warnings en CI para tratar todos los lints como errores. Preferí .map()/.filter() sobre match cuando el resultado es transformación simple. Evitá .clone() innecesario — considerá referencias. Reemplazá unwrap() con ? o manejo explícito en producción.

entries.name: Go — Testing conventions
entries.description: Convenciones del paquete testing de Go: table-driven tests, subtests, helpers y temp dirs.
entries.excerpt: Nombrá los tests TestXxx(t *testing.T) en archivos _test.go. Preferí table-driven tests con subtests t.Run(nombre, ...). Marcá los helpers con t.Helper() para reportar la línea correcta. Usá t.TempDir() para archivos temporales (autolimpieza). Corré go test -race para detectar data races.

entries.name: Angular — Style Guide
entries.description: Guía oficial de estilo de Angular: estructura por feature, inyección de dependencias y componentes.
entries.excerpt: Un archivo por componente/servicio, nombrado feature.tipo.ts (user.component.ts). Usá inject() y standalone components. Mantené los componentes chicos y delegá la lógica a servicios. Activá OnPush change detection para performance. Tipá todo: evitá any.

entries.name: Svelte — Docs & Reactivity
entries.description: Convenciones de Svelte: reactividad con runes, estructura de componentes y stores.
entries.excerpt: En Svelte 5 usá runes ($state, $derived, $effect) para reactividad explícita. Calculá valores derivados con $derived, no con efectos. Un componente por archivo .svelte. Compartí estado con stores. Mantené el markup declarativo y la lógica en <script>.

entries.name: NestJS — Architecture
entries.description: Arquitectura modular de NestJS: módulos, inyección de dependencias, DTOs y separación de capas.
entries.excerpt: Organizá el código en módulos (@Module) con imports y providers explícitos. Inyectá dependencias por constructor. Validá entradas con DTOs + class-validator y un ValidationPipe global. Separá controladores (HTTP) de servicios (lógica). Usá guards para auth e interceptors para cross-cutting concerns.

entries.name: Flask — Patterns
entries.description: Patrones de Flask: application factory, blueprints, configuración y manejo de errores.
entries.excerpt: Usá una application factory (create_app) y blueprints para modularizar. No guardes estado global mutable entre requests. Configurá con app.config y variables de entorno. Manejá errores con @app.errorhandler. En producción usá un WSGI server (gunicorn), nunca el server de desarrollo.

entries.name: pytest — Testing
entries.description: Convenciones de pytest: descubrimiento de tests, fixtures, parametrización y asserts.
entries.excerpt: Nombrá los tests test_*.py con funciones test_*. Usá fixtures (@pytest.fixture) para setup/teardown reutilizable. Parametrizá con @pytest.mark.parametrize en vez de loops. Usá assert plano: pytest reescribe el assert con detalle. Usá tmp_path para archivos temporales.

entries.name: Java — Spring Boot
entries.description: Convenciones de Spring Boot: inyección por constructor, capas, configuración tipada y validación.
entries.excerpt: Preferí inyección por constructor sobre @Autowired en campos. Separá capas con @RestController + @Service + @Repository. Configurá con application.yml y @ConfigurationProperties tipadas. Validá DTOs con @Valid y Bean Validation. Mantené los controladores sin lógica de negocio.

entries.name: Ruby on Rails — Guides
entries.description: Convenciones de Rails: convention over configuration, modelos, controladores y migrations.
entries.excerpt: Seguí convention over configuration: modelos con la lógica, controladores delgados. Usá strong parameters (params.require.permit). Evitá N+1 con includes/eager loading. Validá en el modelo. Cambiá el schema solo con migrations, nunca editando schema.rb a mano. Tareas pesadas con Active Job.

entries.name: PHP — Laravel
entries.description: Convenciones de Laravel: Eloquent, validación con Form Requests, capas y configuración.
entries.excerpt: Usá Eloquent con relaciones explícitas y eager loading (with()) para evitar N+1. Validá con Form Requests. Mantené los controladores delgados y poné la lógica en Services/Actions. Versioná el schema con migrations y seeders. Nunca pongas secrets en el código: usá .env y config().

entries.name: .NET — ASP.NET Core
entries.description: Convenciones de ASP.NET Core: inyección de dependencias, configuración tipada, validación y async.
entries.excerpt: Usá la inyección de dependencias incorporada (builder.Services.Add...). Configurá con appsettings.json + IOptions<T> tipado. Validá con Data Annotations o FluentValidation. Usá async/await en handlers de I/O. Poné los cross-cutting concerns en middleware. Devolvé tipos de resultado claros (Results/ActionResult).

entries.name: Anthropic SDK — Token Optimization (Python)
entries.description: Reduce Anthropic API costs using prompt caching, Message Batches, and token counting in Python.
entries.excerpt: Add cache_control {type: 'ephemeral'} on the last static content block; cache reads cost 0.1x base vs 1.25x for writes, breaking even on the second request. Minimum cacheable size is model-dependent (1024 tokens for Sonnet, 4096 for Haiku); if cache_read_input_tokens and cache_creation_input_tokens are both 0, caching did not occur. Use the Message Batches API (client.messages.batches) for offline work: 50% discount, up to 100k requests per batch. Call client.messages.count_tokens() before sending to measure prompt size — the call is free.

entries.name: Anthropic SDK — Token Optimization (Node.js/TypeScript)
entries.description: Reduce Anthropic API costs using prompt caching, Message Batches, and token counting in TypeScript/JavaScript.
entries.excerpt: Set cache_control {type: 'ephemeral'} on the last static content block; a 5-min TTL costs 1.25x base on write and 0.1x on read, breaking even on the second hit. For a 1-hour TTL (2x write) use {type: 'ephemeral', ttl: '1h'} and place 1h entries before 5m entries. Use client.messages.batches.create() for offline jobs: 50% discount, max 100k requests per batch; stream:true and max_tokens:0 are not allowed in batches. Before sending, call client.messages.countTokens() (free) to verify the prompt fits the context window and estimate cost.

entries.name: Tailwind CSS — Utility-First Styling
entries.description: Utility-first CSS framework for building UIs directly in markup without writing custom CSS.
entries.excerpt: Apply single-purpose utility classes directly in markup instead of writing custom CSS classes. Use @apply only to override third-party libraries or style elements you cannot template — not as a general abstraction. Prefer theme tokens defined via @theme (--color-*, --spacing-*) over arbitrary bracket values like top-[117px]; use arbitrary values only as escape hatches. Always write complete static class names — never build them dynamically (e.g. text-${color}-600) or Tailwind cannot detect them. For reusable patterns, create component abstractions rather than extracting CSS.

entries.name: Prisma ORM — Database Access
entries.description: Type-safe ORM for Node.js with schema-first modeling, migrations, and a declarative query API.
entries.excerpt: Treat schema.prisma as the single source of truth — models, relations and types live there and drive both migrations and the generated client. Use prisma migrate dev in development and prisma migrate deploy in production; never edit a migration after it is applied. Always specify select or include to avoid over-fetching — by default all scalar fields return and no relations. Solve N+1 with nested include/select (a single findMany with include fetches relations in one round trip), not sequential queries. Instantiate PrismaClient as a singleton; in serverless, create it outside the handler.

entries.name: Tokio — Async Runtime
entries.description: Rules for writing correct, efficient async Rust with the Tokio runtime.
entries.excerpt: Never run blocking or CPU-bound work inside async tasks — use spawn_blocking, which offloads to a dedicated thread pool; for indefinite workloads use a separate thread. Restructure code so a std MutexGuard drops before any .await; if you must hold a lock across await, use tokio::sync::Mutex. tokio::spawn requires the future to be Send + 'static — capture owned data with async move. In tokio::select! use only cancellation-safe futures per branch. Bound parallel spawn_blocking tasks with a semaphore to avoid exhausting the thread pool.

entries.name: Serde — Serialization
entries.description: Rules for deriving and customizing Serde Serialize/Deserialize in Rust.
entries.excerpt: Derive Serialize and Deserialize for zero-overhead compile-time impls with no runtime reflection. Use #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] on the container to rename all fields at once; use #[serde(rename = "name")] on a field for one-off overrides. Apply #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] to omit optional fields from output. Use #[serde(borrow)] on &str/&[u8] fields to deserialize without allocation. Apply #[serde(default)] to fill missing fields and #[serde(flatten)] to merge a nested struct's keys into the parent.

entries.name: Pydantic — Data Validation (v2)
entries.description: Validate and serialize Python data by subclassing BaseModel; enforces type safety and constraints at parse time (Pydantic v2).
entries.excerpt: Subclass BaseModel and annotate fields with Python types — Pydantic validates on construction. Use Field(gt=0, min_length=1) for numeric/string constraints; bare annotations enforce no constraint. Call model_validate(dict) and model_validate_json(str) to parse — not the deprecated parse_obj(). Export with model_dump() / model_dump_json(); use mode='json' for JSON-safe primitives. For non-model types (list[int], Union) use TypeAdapter.validate_python(). Enable strict=True via ConfigDict to block implicit str->int coercion.

entries.name: Zod — Schema Validation
entries.description: Define runtime validation schemas in TypeScript with Zod and derive static types from them.
entries.excerpt: Derive TypeScript types from schemas with z.infer<typeof Schema> — never declare the type separately. Use .parse() to throw ZodError on failure and .safeParse() to get { success, data, error } for graceful handling. For async refinements/transforms call .parseAsync()/.safeParseAsync() — sync .parse() throws on async schemas. Add custom rules with .refine() (single error) or .superRefine() (ctx.addIssue for multiple). Use z.coerce.number() to convert string inputs; object schemas strip unknown keys by default — use z.strictObject() to reject them.

entries.name: React Native Conventions
entries.description: React Native framework conventions: core native components vs web DOM, StyleSheet-based styling, virtualized lists, platform-specific code, and the New Architecture.
entries.excerpt: Use core components (View, Text, Image, ScrollView, TextInput), never web DOM elements; all text must be wrapped in <Text>. Define styles with StyleSheet.create using camelCase props; in style arrays the last entry wins. Use FlatList/SectionList over ScrollView for long lists; provide keyExtractor, getItemLayout for fixed-height rows, and wrap renderItem in useCallback with memoized items. Branch platforms with Platform.OS/Platform.select or .ios.js/.android.js files. The New Architecture (Fabric, TurboModules, JSI) is the default since RN 0.76.

entries.name: Expo SDK & Tooling Conventions
entries.description: Expo SDK and tooling conventions: Expo Router file-based routing, app config in app.json/app.config, EAS Build, and development builds vs Expo Go.
entries.excerpt: Use Expo Router file-based routing: files in app/ become routes; index.tsx is the default route, _layout.tsx defines shared navigation (Stack/Tabs), [param] is dynamic (read via useLocalSearchParams), and (group) folders organize without changing the URL. Configure via static app.json or dynamic app.config.ts (export a function for env-based config); read runtime values from Constants.expoConfig. Prefer Expo SDK modules over bare native; use development builds (not Expo Go) once you add native libraries. Build in the cloud with eas build via eas.json profiles.

entries.name: Supabase JS Client (supabase-js v2)
entries.description: Best practices for the supabase-js v2 isomorphic JavaScript client: client initialization, secure querying with Row Level Security, key handling, and auth.
entries.excerpt: Create a single supabase client with createClient(url, key) and reuse it; do not instantiate per request. In the browser use only the anon/publishable key — never the service_role/secret key, which bypasses RLS and must stay server-side. Enable Row Level Security on every table in an exposed schema; with a publishable key no data is returned until you add explicit policies. Select explicit columns with .select('col1, col2') instead of '*', and chain filters like .eq() after it. Authenticate via supabase.auth (signInWithPassword, getUser, onAuthStateChange).

entries.name: Vite Build Tool Conventions
entries.description: Official Vite conventions for project configuration, environment variables, dev server/HMR, static assets, and production builds.
entries.excerpt: Define config in vite.config.ts; wrap with defineConfig for intellisense, or export a function receiving { command, mode } for conditional config. Only env vars prefixed VITE_ are exposed to client code; access them via import.meta.env (never process.env), and keep secrets out since values are bundled. Use the dev server for native ESM and HMR. Import static assets to get resolved URLs (hashed in build), use ?url/?raw suffixes, and reference public/ assets by root-absolute path. Customize the bundle via build.rollupOptions and set base for deploy paths.

entries.name: go-redis v9
entries.description: Redis access in Go with the go-redis v9 client: connection pooling, context-aware commands, pipelines, and missing-key handling.
entries.excerpt: Create one redis.NewClient and reuse it: it is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines and has a built-in connection pool. Pass a context.Context as the first arg to every command for timeout and cancellation. After Get, check `err == redis.Nil` to detect a missing key before treating it as a real error. Batch round-trips with Pipeline/Pipelined (or TxPipeline for atomic MULTI/EXEC) and read results via Exec. Tune Options: PoolSize, DialTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, MaxRetries. Always defer rdb.Close().

entries.name: Go PostgreSQL with database/sql + lib/pq
entries.description: PostgreSQL access in Go using the standard database/sql package with the lib/pq driver: connection pooling, parameterized queries, context, and row handling.
entries.excerpt: Blank-import _ "github.com/lib/pq" to register the driver, then sql.Open("postgres", dsn) once and reuse the *sql.DB (it is a goroutine-safe pool); call db.Ping() to verify. Use $1,$2 ordinal placeholders with params passed separately to prevent SQL injection; never concatenate input. Set SetMaxOpenConns, SetMaxIdleConns, and SetConnMaxLifetime to bound the pool. Prefer QueryRowContext/QueryContext/ExecContext with a context.WithTimeout. defer rows.Close() and check rows.Err() after the Next() loop. lib/pq has no LastInsertId; use INSERT ... RETURNING id with QueryRow.Scan.

entries.name: Code Structure & Flow Auditor
entries.description: Audits codebase structure (organization, cohesion, coupling, layering, dead code) and data/control flow (dependency direction, cycles, context/error propagation) and emits prioritized, evidence-backed findings.
entries.excerpt: Audit structure (module organization, cohesion, coupling, layering, dead code/orphans) and flow (one-way dependency DAG, cycles, inversions, context/error propagation). Build the dep graph with real tooling per stack. Severity: HIGH = cycle/inversion, global mutable state, swallowed errors; MEDIUM = god-file, grab-bag module, dead code; LOW = cosmetic. Do not flag normal wiring hubs or large-but-cohesive modules. Output: Summary, Structure, Flow, Top 3 actions.
